title: Squash logged errors from failed SQL rollbacks
body: Currently we unconditionally roll back transactions after error, even if a commit has already been attempted. Commit is guaranteed to end a transaction, though, whether by successfully committing or by rolling back if that fails. As such, we attempt a double rollback if a transaction fails at commit (for example, for a constraint violation), which doesn't error but does log angry warning messages. Ensure we don't try rolling back after commit runs to prevent this.

comments.body: This doesn't matter much now that we're on bolt, but the errors logged during our unit tests were annoying me
